rationale.summary: This PR replaces the prior integer log rate limiter with a structured random sampling model that spans protocol schema, runtime logging, public configuration APIs, worker/client propagation, documentation, deployment defaults, and regression coverage. The walkthrough shows the change is intentionally broad because the sampling policy must be configured centrally, serialized across process boundaries, and applied consistently on hot logging and access-recorder paths. The overview also identifies hard compatibility breaks in removed public APIs, changed protobuf field type/accessors, and the deleted LogRateLimiter type.

keyPoints.point: The protocol layer changes RegisterClientRspPb field 24 from an int32 log_rate_limit to LogSampleConfigPb and adds heartbeat delivery for the same config.
The runtime logging path replaces LogRateLimiter with LogSampler snapshots, ppm thresholds, categorized decisions, and sampler config serialization.
The access recorder migration is shaped around lazy field construction so sampled-out foreground paths avoid unnecessary string formatting and metadata assembly.
Documentation, deployment defaults, unit tests, performance tests, and system tests were updated to cover the new sampler configuration and propagation model.

critique_ledger.title: Public EmbeddedConfig sampler setters have no direct regression coverage
critique_ledger.rationale: The PR replaces the public embedded-worker LogRateLimit API with RequestSampleRate, AccessSampleRate, and DiagnosticSampleRate, but the added tests exercise LogSampler internals rather than this public path. A typo in the extraArgs key, bad string conversion, or invalid-rate handling would leave embedded workers running the wrong sampling policy without failing any test.

critique_ledger.title: Sampler-disabled access logs take the sampled formatting path
critique_ledger.rationale: When sampling is disabled, every request-context CLIENT/ACCESS record now returns true from IsCurrentRequestLogSampled, so FormatAccessReqMsg appends logSampled:true and builds an extra ostringstream/string before LogPerformance writes the line. That makes the default pass-through mode larger and more allocation-heavy on the access-log hot path, even though the sampler is supposed to add only a lightweight enabled check when disabled.

critique_ledger.title: Access recorder facade repeats the same guard-and-store pattern across every setter
critique_ledger.rationale: The new facade adds dozens of near-identical setters that each manually check ShouldRecordInternal(), mutate one state field, and rely on Record() to remember a matching Fill step. Future access-log fields now require coordinated edits in the state struct, header declaration, cpp setter, and Record() assembly path, so it is easy for a later change to miss the sampled-out guard or silently omit a field from the emitted request parameters.

critique_ledger.title: Cross-process sampling test does not assert the operation or propagation outcome
critique_ledger.rationale: CrossProcessSamplingConsistency claims to verify client-to-worker sampling consistency, but inside the workload it only calls Set/Get/Del and ignores failures and log/sample state. The test would still pass if Set failed for every operation, if Get/Del failed, or if the worker never received the propagated sampling decision.

critique_ledger.title: objectKeysToString overloads duplicate the same truncation and formatting logic
critique_ledger.rationale: The new RepeatedPtrField and C-array overloads reimplement the same bracket/comma/truncation/total handling already present for vector keys. Any future change to access-log key formatting or truncation limits will need to be made in several slightly different loops, which invites inconsistent output across C++, protobuf, and C API call sites.

critique_ledger.title: AccessRecorder public header now depends directly on LogSampler
critique_ledger.rationale: Including log_sampler.h from access_recorder.h couples every AccessRecorder consumer to the sampler implementation even though the public declarations in this header do not expose LogSampler types. That increases rebuild surface and makes the logging subsystem dependency direction harder to reason about as both headers now know about each other’s concepts.

routed_questions.Log sampler configuration and propagatio: Do the new EmbeddedConfig RequestSampleRate, AccessSampleRate, and DiagnosticSampleRate setters emit the exact expected flag names and values, preserve chaining, and reject invalid rates without corrupting prior embedded-worker configuration?
routed_questions.Access recorder lazy migration: When the sampler is disabled, does the access recorder hunk preserve the old pass-through formatting path, or does it still append logSampled:true and allocate an extra formatted request string on the hot path?

per_cohort.routed_question: Do the new EmbeddedConfig RequestSampleRate, AccessSampleRate, and DiagnosticSampleRate setters emit the exact expected flag names and values, preserve chaining, and reject invalid rates without corrupting prior embedded-worker configuration?

When the sampler is disabled, does the access recorder hunk preserve the old pass-through formatting path, or does it still append logSampled:true and allocate an extra formatted request string on the hot path?
